records.ety: [OE. lenen, AS. lǣnan, fr. lǣn loan; akin to G. lehnen to lend. See Loan.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Lent (lnt); p. pr. & vb. n. Lending.]

defs: To allow the custody and use of, on condition of the return of the same; to grant the temporary use of; as, to lend a book; -- opposed to borrow.
quotes: Give me that ring. I'll lend it thee, my dear, but have no power To give it from me.

defs: To allow the possession and use of, on condition of the return of an equivalent in kind; as, to lend money or some article of food.
quotes: Thou shalt not give him thy money upon usury, nor lend him thy victuals for increase.

defs: To afford; to grant or furnish in general; as, to lend assistance; to lend one's name or influence.
quotes: Cato, lend me for a while thy patience.
Mountain lines and distant horizons lend space and largeness to his compositions.

defs: To let for hire or compensation; as, to lend a horse or gig.
notes: ☞ This use of the word is rare in the United States, except with reference to money.
